facts.text: Michael Jackson's 'Thriller' music video, directed by John Landis, runs over 13 minutes and is often considered the greatest music video ever made.
MTV launched on August 1, 1981, with the first video aired being 'Video Killed the Radio Star' by The Buggles.
The 'Thriller' video cost approximately $500,000 to produce in 1983, making it the most expensive music video at the time.
Spike Jonze directed the Beastie Boys' 'Sabotage' video, which parodied 1970s cop shows.
A-ha's 'Take On Me' combined live action with pencil-sketch animation using a technique called rotoscoping.
Michel Gondry is known for his inventive, handcrafted visual style in videos for artists like Björk and The White Stripes.
The first MTV Video Music Awards ceremony was held on September 14, 1984, at Radio City Music Hall in New York.
Hype Williams became one of the most influential hip-hop video directors in the 1990s, known for his fisheye lens shots and lavish sets.
Peter Gabriel's 'Sledgehammer' won a record nine MTV Video Music Awards in 1987.
Beyoncé's 'Single Ladies (Put a Ring on It)' video featured a minimalist black-and-white setup with iconic choreography by JaQuel Knight.
Nirvana's 'Smells Like Teen Spirit' video, directed by Samuel Bayer, helped define the grunge era's visual aesthetic.
David Fincher directed Madonna's 'Express Yourself' and 'Vogue' before becoming a major Hollywood film director.
The most expensive music video ever made is reportedly Michael and Janet Jackson's 'Scream,' which cost around $7 million in 1995.
Psy's 'Gangnam Style' became the first YouTube video to reach one billion views in December 2012.
YouTube was founded in 2005, and music videos quickly became the platform's most-watched content category.
Luis Fonsi's 'Despacito' featuring Daddy Yankee became the fastest video to reach three billion views on YouTube.
Baby Shark Dance by Pinkfong surpassed 'Despacito' to become the most-viewed YouTube video in November 2020.
Sinead O'Connor tore up a photo of Pope John Paul II during a 1992 Saturday Night Live performance, but her 'Nothing Compares 2 U' video was already iconic for its extreme close-up shots.
OK Go's 'Here It Goes Again' treadmill video went viral in 2006 and won a Grammy for Best Short Form Music Video.
Fatboy Slim's 'Weapon of Choice' video featured Christopher Walken dancing through an empty hotel, directed by Spike Jonze.
The White Stripes' 'Fell in Love with a Girl' video by Michel Gondry was animated entirely with LEGO bricks.
Childish Gambino's 'This Is America' video used long takes and symbolic imagery to comment on gun violence and racism in the United States.
Weezer's 'Buddy Holly' video, directed by Spike Jonze, digitally inserted the band into scenes from the TV show Happy Days.
The Prodigy's 'Smack My Bitch Up' video was banned by MTV for its explicit content but later won awards at music video festivals.
Madonna's 'Like a Prayer' video was controversial for its religious imagery, leading Pepsi to cancel a sponsorship deal.
Johnny Cash's 'Hurt' video, directed by Mark Romanek, is widely considered one of the most emotionally powerful music videos ever made.
Michael Jackson's 'Black or White' video premiered simultaneously on MTV, BET, VH1, and Fox in 1991 to an audience of 500 million.
Radiohead's 'Just' video famously ends with a man lying on a sidewalk whose words are shown only in subtitles, leaving the mystery unresolved.
Lauryn Hill's 'Doo Wop (That Thing)' video featured a split screen showing both 1967 and 1998 styles simultaneously.
TLC's 'Waterfalls' video used groundbreaking CGI water effects that were considered revolutionary for 1995.
Missy Elliott's 'The Rain (Supa Dupa Fly)' video, directed by Hype Williams, featured her in an iconic inflatable trash bag suit.
D'Angelo's 'Untitled (How Does It Feel)' video generated headlines for its seemingly nude presentation of the singer.
Jamiroquai's 'Virtual Insanity' video featured moving floors in a white room, achieved through practical effects rather than CGI.
Green Day's 'Boulevard of Broken Dreams' video won Video of the Year at the 2005 MTV VMAs.
The Verve's 'Bitter Sweet Symphony' video featured Richard Ashcroft walking down a London sidewalk, bumping into pedestrians.
Kanye West's 'Runaway' was released as a 35-minute short film in 2010, blurring the line between music video and cinema.
Lady Gaga's 'Bad Romance' was the first video to reach 200 million views on YouTube.
Lady Gaga's 'Telephone' video featuring Beyoncé ran nearly 10 minutes and included elaborate costumes and product placements.
Queen's 'Bohemian Rhapsody' video from 1975 is often cited as one of the earliest true music videos.
Robert Palmer's 'Addicted to Love' video featured a group of identically styled models pretending to play instruments.
Duran Duran's 'Hungry Like the Wolf' was one of the first videos by a non-American act to receive heavy MTV rotation.
The Cars' 'You Might Think' won the first ever MTV Video Music Award for Video of the Year in 1984.
Guns N' Roses' 'November Rain' was one of the most expensive videos of the early 1990s and featured an orchestral performance.
Mark Romanek directed numerous iconic videos including Nine Inch Nails' 'Closer' and Jay-Z's '99 Problems.'
The Gorillaz are a virtual band whose music videos feature animated characters created by Damon Albarn and Jamie Hewlett.
Floria Sigismondi directed videos for Marilyn Manson, David Bowie, and The White Stripes.
BTS's 'Dynamite' broke the YouTube record for most views in 24 hours with over 101 million views upon release in August 2020.
Jonas Åkerlund directed Madonna's 'Ray of Light' video and later went on to direct feature films.
Chris Cunningham's video for Aphex Twin's 'Come to Daddy' was controversial for its disturbing imagery of children wearing the musician's face.
David Bowie's 'Ashes to Ashes' video cost £250,000 in 1980, making it one of the most expensive videos of its time.
Run-DMC's 'Walk This Way' featuring Aerosmith was one of the first rap videos to receive heavy MTV airplay.
Björk's 'All Is Full of Love' video by Chris Cunningham depicted two robots kissing and won multiple awards.
The first Video Music Awards Moonman trophy was designed by Manhattan Design, the same firm that created the MTV logo.
Pearl Jam boycotted making music videos in the mid-1990s as a protest against the commercialization of music.
Soundgarden's 'Black Hole Sun' video featured digitally distorted suburban imagery that became one of the defining visuals of 1990s rock.
Eminem's 'Stan' video, directed by Dr. Dre collaborator Philip Atwell, told a narrative story about an obsessive fan and starred Devon Sawa.
TikTok has become a major platform for music discovery, with songs like Lil Nas X's 'Old Town Road' going viral on the app before charting.
MTV originally stood for Music Television and was dedicated entirely to music videos before shifting to reality programming.
Drake's 'Hotline Bling' video became a massive meme due to his distinctive dance moves in colorful light boxes.
Madonna's 'Justify My Love' was banned from MTV for its sexual content in 1990, but became a commercial success as a video single.
Rihanna's 'We Found Love' video was directed by Melina Matsoukas and depicted a turbulent relationship in vivid colors.
The Weeknd's 'Blinding Lights' video paid homage to 1980s aesthetics with neon lights and Las Vegas settings.
Dua Lipa's 'Levitating' video featured retro space-age visuals inspired by 1970s disco and sci-fi films.
BLACKPINK's 'How You Like That' set a YouTube premiere record with 1.66 million concurrent viewers in June 2020.
Sia's 'Chandelier' video featured then-12-year-old dancer Maddie Ziegler and has over three billion YouTube views.
The Beatles' films A Hard Day's Night and Help! are considered precursors to the modern music video format.
David Bowie's 'Space Oddity' was broadcast by the BBC during the Apollo 11 moon landing coverage in 1969.
Devo's quirky, art-influenced videos in the late 1970s helped establish the music video as an art form before MTV existed.
Talking Heads' 'Once in a Lifetime' video featured David Byrne's iconic jerky movements, influenced by televangelists.
Bonnie Tyler's 'Total Eclipse of the Heart' video, directed by Russell Mulcahy, had a gothic, surreal style set in a boarding school.
Prince's 'When Doves Cry' was the first music video to feature the artist without any accompanying band.
Spike Jonze's real name is Adam Spiegel, and he transitioned from music videos to directing films like Being John Malkovich.
Van Halen's 'Jump' video showcased the band performing with minimal effects, relying on their energetic stage presence.
Cyndi Lauper's 'Girls Just Want to Have Fun' video featured her real mother and helped establish her quirky visual persona.
Whitney Houston's 'I Will Always Love You' video was shot largely as a simple performance piece and became one of the best-selling singles of all time.
The video for 'Sledgehammer' by Peter Gabriel used Aardman Animations, the studio that later created Wallace & Gromit.
Paula Abdul's 'Opposites Attract' video featured the singer dancing with an animated cat named MC Skat Kat.
MTV initially faced criticism for rarely playing videos by Black artists until Michael Jackson's videos broke the color barrier.
Janet Jackson's 'Rhythm Nation' video featured military-inspired choreography that became widely imitated.
The MTV Europe Music Awards were first held in Berlin on November 24, 1994.
R.E.M.'s 'Losing My Religion' video, directed by Tarsem Singh, drew heavily on the paintings of Caravaggio.
Massive Attack's 'Teardrop' video featured a CGI fetus singing inside a womb.
Portishead's music videos helped define the visual style of trip-hop in the 1990s.
OutKast's 'Hey Ya!' video showed André 3000 playing every member of a fictional band on a TV show, inspired by The Ed Sullivan Show.
Taylor Swift's 'Bad Blood' video featured numerous celebrity cameos and a cinematic action-movie aesthetic.
The Beastie Boys' '(You Gotta) Fight for Your Right (To Party!)' video depicted a house party and became an MTV staple.
Nine Inch Nails' 'Closer' video directed by Mark Romanek was heavily censored by MTV for its provocative imagery.
Tool's 'Sober' video used stop-motion animation with clay figures created by Adam Jones.
Aphex Twin's 'Windowlicker' video by Chris Cunningham featured the artist's face superimposed on bikini-clad women.
Kendrick Lamar's 'HUMBLE.' video, directed by Dave Meyers and the Little Homies, used striking visual compositions inspired by classical art.
The concept of a music video dates back to the 1920s and 1930s, when short musical films called 'soundies' were played on visual jukeboxes.
Frank Ocean's 'Nikes' video served as the visual introduction to his album Blonde and featured experimental imagery.
Kanye West interrupted Taylor Swift's VMA acceptance speech in 2009, creating one of the most infamous moments in awards show history.
Tyler, the Creator directed many of his own music videos, establishing a distinctive colorful and surreal visual style.
Billie Eilish's 'Bad Guy' video used quirky, low-budget practical effects and helped define her offbeat visual brand.
Lil Nas X's 'Montero (Call Me by Your Name)' video was controversial for its depiction of the artist sliding down a pole to hell.
The Cranberries' 'Zombie' video addressed the conflict in Northern Ireland and featured powerful war imagery.
Dire Straits' 'Money for Nothing' was one of the first music videos to use computer-generated imagery extensively in 1985.
U2's 'One' video had three different versions directed by three different directors.
Beyoncé's visual album Lemonade premiered on HBO in 2016, with each song accompanied by a cinematic music video.
Alanis Morissette's 'Ironic' video featured the singer playing four characters in a car.
No Doubt's 'Don't Speak' video dramatized growing tensions within the band and was one of the most-played videos on MTV in 1997.
Backstreet Boys' 'Everybody (Backstreet's Back)' video was a horror-themed production with elaborate sets and costumes.
NSYNC's 'Bye Bye Bye' video featured the boy band as marionettes trying to break free from their strings.
George Michael's 'Freedom! '90' video featured supermodels Naomi Campbell, Linda Evangelista, Christy Turlington, Cindy Crawford, and Tatjana Patitz instead of the singer himself.
The Spice Girls' 'Wannabe' video was filmed in one take at the St Pancras Grand Hotel in London.
Christina Aguilera's 'Dirrty' video was controversial for its provocative content and marked a major image shift for the singer.
Destiny's Child's 'Say My Name' video featured the group in multiple matching outfits in color-coordinated rooms.
Justin Timberlake's 'Cry Me a River' video implied themes of infidelity referencing his breakup with Britney Spears.
50 Cent's 'In Da Club' video was set in a laboratory where the rapper was being manufactured as a hit machine.
Blind Melon's 'No Rain' video featured the iconic 'Bee Girl' character, played by child actress Heather DeLoach.
Nelly's 'Hot in Herre' video helped popularize the early 2000s party video aesthetic.
Lady Gaga's meat dress at the 2010 VMAs became one of the most talked-about fashion moments in music history.
The Black Eyed Peas' 'Where Is the Love?' video featured the group walking through Los Angeles posting question mark stickers.
Outkast's 'Roses' video featured an elaborate narrative set at a formal event that descends into chaos.
Coldplay's 'The Scientist' video was filmed with Chris Martin singing the lyrics backward so it could be reversed in post-production.
The White Stripes' 'Seven Nation Army' video used an infinite corridor effect created by Michel Gondry.
Marilyn Manson's 'The Beautiful People' video was banned in several countries for its disturbing visuals.
Gorillaz's 'Feel Good Inc.' video seamlessly blended 2D animation with 3D backgrounds and won a Grammy.
Daft Punk's 'Around the World' video by Michel Gondry featured five groups of dancers representing different instruments.
Fatboy Slim's 'Praise You' video, directed by Spike Jonze, featured a fake dance troupe performing outside a movie theater.
Massive Attack's 'Unfinished Sympathy' video was a single tracking shot following singer Shara Nelson walking down a street in Los Angeles.
Vince Staples' 'Big Fish' video used a continuous overhead water shot as a visual metaphor for the song's themes.
FKA twigs' 'Cellophane' video featured the artist performing on a pole and was directed by Andrew Thomas Huang.
Janelle Monáe's music videos form a connected narrative about an android named Cindi Mayweather.
Britney Spears performed with a live python around her neck during the 2001 VMAs.
Radiohead released 'House of Cards' using 3D data visualization techniques instead of traditional cameras.
Cardi B's 'WAP' featuring Megan Thee Stallion broke YouTube records with 26 million views in its first 24 hours.
Bruno Mars' 'Uptown Funk' video paid tribute to 1980s and 1990s funk aesthetics and has over five billion YouTube views.
Ed Sheeran's 'Shape of You' video featured the singer training as a sumo wrestler and has been viewed over six billion times on YouTube.
Fiona Apple's 'Criminal' video directed by Mark Romanek won Best Direction at the 1997 VMAs.
Mark Ronson's 'Uptown Funk' featuring Bruno Mars was directed by Bruno Mars and Cameron Duddy.
VEVO was launched in 2009 as a joint venture between major record labels to host official music videos on YouTube.
PSY followed up 'Gangnam Style' with 'Gentleman,' which also broke YouTube records upon release.
Wiz Khalifa's 'See You Again' from Furious 7 became one of the most-viewed YouTube videos due to its tribute to Paul Walker.
LMFAO's 'Party Rock Anthem' video featured a zombie-dance-apocalypse concept inspired by 28 Days Later.
Carly Rae Jepsen's 'Call Me Maybe' became a global phenomenon partly through viral fan-made videos.
Britney Spears' '...Baby One More Time' video set in a school was originally conceived with a different concept before Spears suggested the schoolgirl outfit.
Pharrell Williams' 'Happy' was released as the world's first 24-hour music video, with different performers dancing throughout the day.
Robin Thicke's 'Blurred Lines' had two versions, with the unrated version generating controversy for its nudity.
Katy Perry's 'Roar' video set in a jungle has over three billion YouTube views.
Ariana Grande's 'Thank U, Next' video parodied iconic teen movies and broke YouTube's 24-hour viewing record in 2018.
BTS became the first K-pop group to win a VMA, taking Best Group in 2019.
Usher's 'Yeah!' video captured the mid-2000s club aesthetic and became one of the most iconic R&B visuals.
BLACKPINK's 'DDU-DU DDU-DU' was the first K-pop group music video to surpass one billion YouTube views.
Shakira and Jennifer Lopez's 2020 Super Bowl halftime performance video became one of the most-watched on YouTube that year.
Lil Nas X's 'Old Town Road' went viral on TikTok before topping the Billboard Hot 100 for a record 19 weeks.
Doja Cat's 'Say So' became a hit largely through a TikTok dance created by user Haley Sharpe.
Megan Thee Stallion's 'Savage' remix featuring Beyoncé went viral after a TikTok dance challenge.
Jason Derulo became one of TikTok's biggest music stars, using the platform to promote his singles.
Shakira's 'Hips Don't Lie' video has been viewed over two billion times on YouTube.
VH1's 'Pop Up Video' added trivia bubbles to music videos and ran from 1996 to 2002, then was briefly revived in 2011.
BET's '106 & Park' was a music video countdown show that ran from 2000 to 2014.
MTV's 'Total Request Live' (TRL) was a daily music video countdown show that became a cultural phenomenon from 1998 to 2008.
The Buggles' 'Video Killed the Radio Star' was prophetic about MTV's impact on the music industry.
Michael Jackson's 'Bad' video was directed by Martin Scorsese and filmed in a New York City subway station.
The Chemical Brothers' 'Star Guitar' video by Michel Gondry synced passing scenery from a train window to the beat of the music.
Michael Jackson's 'Smooth Criminal' video debuted the anti-gravity lean, achieved with specially designed shoes and a stage mechanism.
Prince's Super Bowl halftime performance in 2007 is widely regarded as the greatest ever, with his 'Purple Rain' segment performed in actual rain.
Michael Jackson's 'Remember the Time' video featured Eddie Murphy, Iman, and Magic Johnson in an ancient Egyptian setting.
Madonna's 'Material Girl' video was inspired by Marilyn Monroe's 'Diamonds Are a Girl's Best Friend' performance.
Janet Jackson's 'Nasty' video pioneered the gritty street-dance aesthetic that influenced pop and hip-hop videos for years.
Jimi Hendrix's performance footage of 'Purple Haze' is considered proto-music-video material from the 1960s.
Arcade Fire's 'The Wilderness Downtown' was an interactive music video that used Google Chrome and Google Maps to personalize the experience.
Bob Dylan's 'Subterranean Homesick Blues' clip from 1965. featuring Dylan holding cue cards, is one of the earliest conceptual music videos.
MTV launched MTV2 in 1996 to continue playing music videos as the main channel shifted toward reality programming.
The Residents released some of the first intentionally artistic music videos in the late 1970s.
ABBA frequently used promotional film clips for their songs, which helped popularize them internationally without extensive touring.
Vertical music videos became common after 2018, designed specifically for smartphone viewing on platforms like Snapchat and IGTV.
Virtual reality music videos allow viewers to look around a 360-degree environment, with Gorillaz and Björk among early adopters.
Adele's 'Hello' was the first video to reach one billion views on Vevo.
Travis Scott's 'Astronomical' concert in Fortnite was watched by 12.3 million concurrent players in April 2020.
Lil Dicky's 'Earth' video featured animated versions of numerous celebrities voicing different animals.
Guns N' Roses' 'Estranged' video cost $4 million in 1993 and featured dolphins and underwater sequences.
Aerosmith's 'Cryin,' 'Amazing,' and 'Crazy' videos formed a loose trilogy starring Alicia Silverstone.
Red Hot Chili Peppers' 'Californication' video was styled like a video game with the band members as playable characters.
Gotye's 'Somebody That I Used to Know' video featured body paint that merged the singers with a painted backdrop.
Foo Fighters' 'Everlong' video, directed by Michel Gondry, depicted a surreal dream sequence.
Nicki Minaj's 'Anaconda' video broke the Vevo record for most views in 24 hours when it was released in 2014.
Smashing Pumpkins' 'Tonight, Tonight' video used early cinema techniques inspired by Georges Méliès' A Trip to the Moon.
Metallica's 'Enter Sandman' video used dark, nightmarish imagery and helped bring metal videos to mainstream MTV.
Iron Maiden was one of the first metal bands to create elaborate narrative music videos in the early 1980s.
Mötley Crüe's 'Home Sweet Home' video is credited with helping create the power ballad video genre on MTV.
Miley Cyrus' 'Wrecking Ball' video directed by Terry Richardson featured the singer swinging naked on a wrecking ball.
Bon Jovi's 'Livin' on a Prayer' video features the band performing on a warehouse stage and is one of the 1980s' most iconic rock videos.
Twisted Sister's 'We're Not Gonna Take It' video featured a rebellious teenager launching his father through walls.
AC/DC's 'Thunderstruck' live performance videos became some of the most-watched rock clips on YouTube.
The Rolling Stones' 'Love Is Strong' video used forced perspective to make the band appear as giants walking through New York City.
Peter Gabriel was among the first major artists to embrace music video as an art form, commissioning innovative directors for nearly every single.
TikTok's short-form video format has influenced how artists create music, with many songs designed to become viral dance challenges.
Kate Bush's 'Wuthering Heights' video from 1978 featured her distinctive interpretive dance and predated MTV by three years.
MTV first aired 'Yo! MTV Raps' in 1988, bringing hip-hop videos to a mainstream television audience.
Grace Jones' music videos in the early 1980s pushed boundaries with avant-garde fashion and visual design.
Eurythmics' 'Sweet Dreams (Are Made of This)' video featured Annie Lennox's androgynous look, which became iconic.
Van Morrison rarely appeared in music videos throughout his career, preferring live performance footage.
Tom Petty's 'Don't Come Around Here No More' video featured an Alice in Wonderland theme directed by Jeff Stein.
The 'Renegade' dance on TikTok, originally created by Jalaiah Harmon, brought attention to how creators are credited for viral choreography.
Bruce Springsteen's 'Dancing in the Dark' video featured a young Courteney Cox being pulled from the audience to dance with the Boss.
MTV's 'Headbangers Ball' was dedicated to heavy metal music videos and ran from 1987 to 1995, then was briefly revived.
Phil Collins' 'In the Air Tonight' video is known for its moody lighting and dramatic drum-fill moment.
Depeche Mode's 'Enjoy the Silence' video featured Dave Gahan wandering landscapes in a king's robe carrying a deck chair, directed by Anton Corbijn.
Anton Corbijn's stark, atmospheric visual style defined the look of videos for U2, Depeche Mode, and Joy Division.
Samuel Bayer, who directed Nirvana's 'Smells Like Teen Spirit,' initially envisioned a much darker video before the band's input shaped the final result.
Carson Daly hosted TRL and became famous for the screaming crowds that gathered in Times Square to watch the show.
Jonathan Glazer directed Radiohead's 'Karma Police' video before going on to direct films like Under the Skin.
Dave Meyers is one of the most prolific music video directors, with hundreds of credits spanning Missy Elliott, P!nk, and Kendrick Lamar.
Sophie Muller has directed numerous videos for artists like Eurythmics, No Doubt, and Sade, bringing a consistent visual elegance.
Diane Martel directed Robin Thicke's 'Blurred Lines' and Miley Cyrus' 'We Can't Stop,' both of which generated significant controversy.
Director X (Julien Christian Lutz) is known for his work with Drake, Rihanna, and Usher in hip-hop and R&B videos.
Michael Jackson trademarked many of his dance moves, and the moonwalk first appeared publicly during a 1983 television performance.
Jake Nava directed Beyoncé's 'Single Ladies,' 'Crazy in Love,' and 'Beautiful Liar.'
Missy Elliott's inflatable suit from 'The Rain' became one of the most recognizable costumes in music video history.
Hype Williams was known for using anamorphic lenses and vibrant color palettes that defined mainstream hip-hop visuals in the late 1990s.
Nigel Dick directed over 300 music videos for artists including Guns N' Roses, Britney Spears, and Oasis.
Russell Mulcahy directed numerous early MTV videos including Duran Duran's 'Rio' and later directed the film Highlander.
Michael Jackson's 'Thriller' was added to the National Film Registry at the Library of Congress in 2009.
Prince performed in the rain during his iconic Super Bowl halftime show in 2007, but his 'Purple Rain' video had already cemented rain as his visual signature.
Beyoncé holds the record for most VMA wins by a solo artist.
The 2020 MTV VMAs were held with limited or no audience due to the COVID-19 pandemic.
Green screen technology revolutionized music video production in the 2000s, allowing artists to appear in any environment.
Motion capture technology used in video games has been adopted by music video directors to create realistic animated characters.
LED screens and projection mapping have become common tools in modern music videos, allowing dynamic backgrounds to be changed in real time.
The concept of the lyric video, featuring animated text synced to music, became popular on YouTube in the 2010s as a way to release content before official videos.
Deepfake technology has raised ethical concerns in music videos, with some artists using AI-generated faces in their visuals.
Lauryn Hill won the Grammy for Best Music Video for 'Doo Wop (That Thing)' in 1999.
Bullet time, popularized by The Matrix, was used in several music videos in the early 2000s.
Stop-motion animation has been used in music videos from Peter Gabriel's 'Sledgehammer' to Radiohead's 'There There.'
Rotoscoping, as used in A-ha's 'Take On Me,' involves tracing over live-action footage frame by frame to create animation.
One-take music videos, filmed in a single continuous shot, require extensive rehearsal and choreography.
Rammstein's music videos frequently feature pyrotechnics and industrial imagery consistent with their live shows.
Split-screen techniques have been used in music videos since the 1960s to show multiple perspectives simultaneously.
BTS' 'Butter' music video required just 20 hours and 54 minutes to hit 100 million YouTube views.
The fish-eye lens became synonymous with 1990s hip-hop videos, largely due to Hype Williams' influence.
Slow-motion photography in music videos gained popularity in the 2010s, with directors using Phantom high-speed cameras.
Drone footage revolutionized aerial shots in music videos, making sweeping landscape shots affordable for independent artists.
Average music video budgets in the 2020s range from a few thousand dollars for independent artists to several million for top pop stars.
Weezer's 'Undone – The Sweater Song' was one of the simplest hit videos of the 1990s, featuring the band playing on a set while objects moved around them.
YouTube's Content ID system automatically identifies copyrighted music in videos and allows rights holders to monetize or block them.
Music video commissioners at record labels select directors and manage budgets for official video releases.
The UK's 'Top of the Pops' TV show was pivotal in promoting music videos in Britain from the 1960s through the 2000s.
Australia's 'Countdown' TV show was an influential music video program in the 1970s and 1980s.
Japan's music video industry developed its own aesthetic, with J-pop videos often featuring elaborate costumes and storylines.
Def Leppard's 'Pour Some Sugar on Me' video became one of the defining hair metal videos of the 1980s.
K-pop music videos are known for their high production values, precise choreography, and colorful sets.
Beyoncé dropped her self-titled visual album with no prior announcement on December 13, 2013, changing how albums are released.
Latin music videos, especially reggaeton clips, became dominant on YouTube's most-viewed lists in the late 2010s.
Afrobeats music videos have gained global visibility through YouTube, with artists like Burna Boy and Wizkid reaching billions of views.
Nigerian music video production has grown rapidly, with Lagos becoming a major hub for African music video filming.
Brazilian funk (baile funk) music videos often feature street scenes and dance battles from Rio de Janeiro's favelas.
David Bowie collaborated with multiple video directors throughout his career, treating each video as a unique artistic statement.
Bad Bunny's music videos frequently challenge gender norms and incorporate Puerto Rican cultural references.
Rosalía's 'Malamente' video drew on Spanish flamenco traditions while incorporating modern visual effects.
J Balvin's 'Mi Gente' video became one of the most-viewed Latin music videos on YouTube.
Choreographer Parris Goebel created the choreography for Justin Bieber's 'Sorry' video, featuring the Royal Family dance crew.
Michael Jackson hired choreographer Michael Peters for the 'Thriller' and 'Beat It' videos.
Culture Club's 'Karma Chameleon' video was set on a Mississippi riverboat and featured Boy George in his trademark colorful style.
Bob Fosse's choreographic style influenced many music video choreographers, from 'Single Ladies' to Paula Abdul's work.
Coolio's 'Gangsta's Paradise' video featured Michelle Pfeiffer and was tied to the film Dangerous Minds.
The 'Single Ladies' dance was inspired by a 1969 Bob Fosse routine called 'Mexican Breakfast' performed on The Ed Sullivan Show.
Choreographed flash mobs became a music video trend in the late 2000s, with T-Mobile and other brands funding viral dance videos.
The 'WAP' dance challenge on TikTok generated millions of user-created videos in 2020.
Ciara's 'Goodies' video featured intricate choreography by The Hi-Hat that helped establish her as a dance-focused pop artist.
Tears for Fears' 'Head Over Heels' was filmed in the Bodleian Library at Oxford University.
Chris Brown's music videos regularly showcase his dancing abilities, drawing comparisons to Michael Jackson.
Taylor Swift re-recorded her music video for 'Love Story (Taylor's Version)' as part of her effort to own her master recordings.
Missy Elliott's 'Lose Control' video featured krumping, helping bring the LA street dance form to mainstream attention.
Nine Inch Nails' 'Happiness in Slavery' was banned for depicting a man being tortured by a machine.
The Rolling Stones' 'Like a Rolling Stone' lyric video was innovative for allowing viewers to switch between different TV channels within the video.
Sinead O'Connor's 'Nothing Compares 2 U' close-up shot with a real tear became one of the most iconic images in music video history.
Joseph Kahn has directed videos for Taylor Swift, Britney Spears, and Eminem, often using cinematic production values.
Eric Prydz's 'Call on Me' video was controversial for its aerobics-themed sexual content but became a massive hit in Europe.
2Pac's 'Hit 'Em Up' video directly threatened rival rappers and was never played in regular MTV rotation.
M.I.A.'s 'Born Free' video, depicting violence against redheaded people, was removed from YouTube for its graphic content.
Die Antwoord's provocative music videos brought South African zef culture to a global audience.
Jack White directed several White Stripes videos under the pseudonym 'Ill Whip Willie.'
Francis Lawrence directed several iconic videos for Aerosmith and Lady Gaga before directing The Hunger Games films.
YouTube's premiere feature allows artists to debut music videos at a scheduled time with a live chat, mimicking a communal viewing experience.
Michael Jackson's 'Thriller' music video premiered on MTV on December 2, 1983.
Spotify launched its own music video feature in select markets, competing with YouTube for visual music content.
Apple Music has invested in exclusive music video content, including visual albums and original productions.
Amazon Music began offering music videos in 2020 as part of its streaming service.
The rise of short-form video platforms like TikTok, YouTube Shorts, and Instagram Reels has fragmented how music videos are consumed.
The VMA Moonman trophy was temporarily renamed the Moon Person in 2017 to be more gender-inclusive.
Interactive music videos allow viewers to make choices that affect the narrative, similar to choose-your-own-adventure stories.
Bob Dylan's 'Like a Rolling Stone' interactive video let viewers flip through TV channels, each featuring actors lip-syncing the lyrics.
Björk's 'Stonemilker' was released as a 360-degree virtual reality video viewable with a VR headset.
Childish Gambino's 'Feels Like Summer' video featured animated caricatures of dozens of contemporary musicians.
Tyler, the Creator's 'EARFQUAKE' video featured the rapper surrounded by pastel colors and a burning vehicle.
Madonna has won 20 MTV Video Music Awards, more than any other artist.
Post Malone's 'Rockstar' video featured elaborate film-noir styling and generated over two billion YouTube views.
YouTube was officially launched to the public on December 15, 2005.
Travis Scott's 'SICKO MODE' video used multiple visual styles to match the song's several beat switches.
Harry Styles' 'Watermelon Sugar' video captured a nostalgic summer aesthetic and won the 2021 Grammy for Best Music Video.
Dua Lipa's 'Don't Start Now' video was filmed at a nightclub and captured the retro-disco revival aesthetic.
The Weeknd maintained his 'After Hours' character with a bandaged face across multiple music videos and public appearances.
Eminem's 'Without Me' video parodied several public figures and won the VMA for Video of the Year in 2002.
Lizzo's 'Juice' video paid homage to 1980s fitness culture and morning TV shows.
The first Grammy Award for Best Music Video was given on February 28, 1984.
Olivia Rodrigo's 'Drivers License' became a viral hit partly through emotional fan reaction videos on TikTok.
Machine Gun Kelly's 'Bloody Valentine' video starred Megan Fox and marked his pivot from rap to pop-punk.
Doja Cat's 'Kiss Me More' featuring SZA used a sci-fi planet setting and holographic visuals.
Glass Animals' 'Heat Waves' went viral on TikTok months after release, eventually reaching number one on the Billboard Hot 100.
AI-generated music videos emerged in the 2020s, with tools like Runway and DALL-E being used to create visuals from text prompts.
Sam Smith and Kim Petras' 'Unholy' video featured burlesque-inspired visuals that generated social media buzz.
Ice Spice's rise was fueled by TikTok virality, with 'Munch (Feelin' U)' becoming a platform sensation.
SZA's 'Kill Bill' video drew on Quentin Tarantino's film aesthetic, matching the song's revenge narrative.
Beyoncé's 'Renaissance' visual album was released as a concert film in 2023, continuing her tradition of visual albums.
Vampire Weekend's 'Harmony Hall' video was filmed vertically, anticipating the shift toward mobile-first content.
The longest music video ever made is for Pharrell Williams' '24 Hours of Happy,' running a full 24 hours with different dancers.
YouTube Music became the primary destination for discovering new music videos. surpassing traditional TV channels.
Queen performed their legendary Live Aid set at Wembley Stadium on July 13, 1985, which became one of the most-watched concert videos ever.
The rise of fan-made music videos and edits on platforms like YouTube has created a new form of grassroots music promotion.
Reaction videos, where people film themselves watching music videos for the first time, have become their own genre on YouTube.
Frank Ocean's 'Endless' visual album featured a continuous shot of the artist building a staircase in a warehouse.
Solange's 'A Seat at the Table' was accompanied by performance art-style videos that explored Black identity.
Music videos are typically shot in one to three days, though elaborate productions can take weeks.
Janelle Monáe's 'Dirty Computer' emotion picture was a 46-minute narrative film accompanying her 2018 album.
The 1975's 'Sincerity Is Scary' video featured a full dance routine by lead singer Matty Healy with professional dancers.
Twenty One Pilots created an interconnected narrative across multiple album cycles, with music videos continuing an ongoing storyline.
Tierra Whack's 'Whack World' released 15 one-minute music videos as a single 15-minute short film on YouTube.
Bo Burnham's 'Inside' special blurred the line between music video, comedy special, and film during the pandemic.
Many music video directors started their careers making skate videos, including Spike Jonze.
Lin-Manuel Miranda's Hamilton cast performed 'Yorktown' in a music video style for the filmed version on Disney+.
Lady Gaga's 'Bad Romance' video was released on November 23, 2009, and went on to win seven MTV VMAs.
The term 'music video' wasn't widely used until MTV popularized it in the 1980s; before that, they were called promotional clips or films.
Tony Bennett and Lady Gaga's collaborative videos showcased a classic jazz aesthetic rarely seen in modern music videos.
Weird Al Yankovic's parody music videos became almost as famous as the originals, with MTV regularly airing them.
OK Go's 'This Too Shall Pass' Rube Goldberg machine video was filmed in a single take and required 60 attempts.
Bollywood song-and-dance sequences have influenced global music video aesthetics, with M.I.A. and other artists incorporating Indian visual elements.
Sia consistently hid her face in music videos, using dancer Maddie Ziegler as her visual avatar.
Nirvana's 'Smells Like Teen Spirit' video premiered on MTV on September 29, 1991.
The concept of a 'making of' featurette for music videos became popular in the 2000s, adding behind-the-scenes content.
Many music video directors now release director's cuts that restore footage trimmed for broadcast or streaming.
The Grammy Award for Best Music Video was first awarded in 1984 to Duran Duran's 'Girls on Film / Hungry Like the Wolf.'
YouTube Rewind, an annual compilation video, was often topped by music moments and became a cultural event before being discontinued.
Reggaeton pioneer Daddy Yankee's 'Gasolina' video helped bring Latin urban music to a global audience in 2004.
The Super Bowl halftime show has become one of the most-watched musical performances globally, with its videos viewed billions of times online.
Live-streamed concerts became essential during the COVID-19 pandemic, with artists like BTS, Dua Lipa, and The Weeknd performing virtual shows.
Juice WRLD's 'Lucid Dreams' video used psychedelic visual effects and has been viewed over two billion times.
XXXTentacion's 'SAD!' video depicted his own funeral in a controversial artistic choice released after the rapper's death.
Pop Smoke's posthumous videos maintained his dark, cinematic Brooklyn drill aesthetic.
Choreographer Wade Robson created iconic routines for NSYNC and Britney Spears videos in the late 1990s and early 2000s.
Lana Del Rey's 'Video Games' was made with found footage and webcam clips, proving that high budgets aren't necessary for impact.
Childish Gambino's 'This Is America' was released on May 5, 2018, and immediately went viral for its powerful social commentary.
FKA twigs' 'Water Me' video featured an extreme close-up of her face with an enlarged CG teardrop.
Anderson .Paak's 'Come Down' video paid homage to 1970s soul performances with its retro staging.
Perfume Genius' 'Queen' video challenged norms around masculinity and beauty in its quietly defiant imagery.
Grimes directed and edited many of her own music videos, maintaining creative control over her visual output.
Dance challenges on TikTok have become a primary vehicle for promoting new songs, with choreography often created by fans rather than professionals.
Charli XCX's 'Boys' video featured male celebrities playing with puppies, cooking, and posing in a reversal of typical gender roles in pop videos.
Rina Sawayama's 'XS' video satirized consumerism with an infomercial aesthetic.
Vimeo became an alternative platform for artistic music videos that prioritized visual quality over view counts.
Music video colorists and color graders have become increasingly recognized for their role in establishing the visual mood of a video.
Cinematographer Malik Sayeed brought a film-quality look to music videos for artists like Jay-Z and Nas.
Prodigy's 'Firestarter' video featuring Keith Flint's wild appearance in a tunnel was banned by the BBC.
The use of film stock versus digital cameras remains a creative choice in music videos, with some directors preferring the grain of 16mm film.
A-ha's 'Take On Me' reached number one on the Billboard Hot 100 on October 19, 1985.
YouTube's algorithm tends to favor music videos due to their high replay value, contributing to their dominance on the platform.
The concept of 'video albums' has led to some artists releasing more visual content than traditional audio-only tracks.
Vinyl record packaging in the 1960s and 1970s served a similar function to music videos, providing a visual companion to the music.
The 'YouTube era' of music videos began roughly around 2005-2006, democratizing music video distribution.
Robbie Williams' 'Rock DJ' video showed him stripping off not just clothes but skin and muscle in a CGI sequence.
Independent artists can now create and distribute music videos without label support, using smartphones and free editing software.
Psy's 'Gangnam Style' was released on July 15, 2012, and became the first YouTube video to reach one billion views.
Cole Bennett's Lyrical Lemonade YouTube channel became a major platform for launching rap artists through visually striking music videos.
Cole Bennett directed breakout videos for Juice WRLD, Lil Pump, and Ski Mask the Slump God.
Wyclef Jean's 'Gone Till November' video told a narrative about immigration and separation.
Lauryn Hill's 'Everything Is Everything' video featured a giant turntable spinning New York City.
YouTube Premium (originally YouTube Red) funded original music content and exclusive video premieres starting in 2015.
A Tribe Called Quest's 'Can I Kick It?' video was filmed in black and white on the streets of New York.
De La Soul's 'Me Myself and I' video parodied conformity and became an early hip-hop visual classic.
Facebook Watch and Instagram TV (IGTV) offered platforms for music video distribution before being phased out or restructured.
The Weeknd's 'After Hours' short film experience was available as a virtual reality ride in select locations.
Billie Eilish's 'Bury a Friend' was shot from a horror perspective with Eilish being dragged under a bed.
Megan Thee Stallion's 'Body' video featured body-positive imagery and cameos from several celebrities.
The Weeknd's 'Save Your Tears' featured prosthetic makeup that made his face appear surgically altered, commenting on beauty standards.
The first music video shot entirely on an iPhone was Bent Knee's 'Thank You for Hurting Me' in 2016.
The concept of the 'visual album,' where every song gets a video, was popularized by Beyoncé's self-titled 2013 album.
Rosalía's 'El Mal Querer' visual album drew on medieval Spanish literature and flamenco tradition.
Music videos for Broadway musicals have become marketing tools, with shows like Dear Evan Hansen releasing professional clips online.
